rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM HWONG ET AL VS. DENISE J. BOOKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Declare Judgemnt Null And Void And Recall Writ Of Possession; Memo Of P&A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday March 26, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT DENISE BOOKER Notice Of Motion To Declare Judgemnt Null And Void And Recall Writ Of Possession DENIED. Defendant failed to comply with the Court's order. Defendant fails to cite any authority whatsoever for the proposition that a business check of a "very reputable and recognized rental assistance agency" qualifies as "certified funds." No statutes, codes, or cases are cited by the moving party. Factually, it is not clear how and why Plaintiff is supposed to know that RADCo's business account is covered by overdraft protection; or research what RADCo and its mission are. Had counsel admitted her fault regarding her factual misunderstanding as to RADCo checks, the Court could have treated this as a CCP 473(b) motion for mandatory relief. However, counsel did not do so, and instead sought sanctions against Plaintiff. The Court notes that while Plaintiff was within his right to reject a third party business check when the order required cash or certified funds, the decision to obtain the writ was rushed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday March 26, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT CHRISTINE RIOS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege compliance with or exemption from SFRO. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: THE UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES VS. REGINALD "LATE NIGHT" BARNES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday March 26, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT REGINALD BARNES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Notice is based, inter alia, on material non-compliance with the lease (failure to pay rent). Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that if one of multiple bases for the Notice ceases to exit, the notice becomes false. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES VS. IBTIHAJ SURAYYA HAMMOND ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday March 26, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT IBTIHAJ HAMMOND DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend. Notice is fatally defective. Notice fails to provide "the name ¿of the person to whom the rent payment shall be made." No name of a person, natural or corporate is provided. The Court notes that the name of a person to whom possession is to be surrendered is provided. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CARL POON VS. MICHELLE BANCROFT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday March 26, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHELLE BANCROFT MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Hearing Required. Defendant to present to the Court by no later than 9:00 a.m. on March 26, 2019 copies of the checks/money order, etc. (copy of the money order or receipt for purchase of the money order; or copy of a personal check with a copy of a bank statement showing availability of funds on February 4, 2019) allegedly tendered on February 4, 2019. = (501/CFH)
